reviewTitle: Not very impressed with these sticky traps
reviewDescription: I purchased the large rectangular sticky traps before, but since the problem was fungus gnats that I brought home in a plant from a big box store that then infested the soil of other plants, I decided to give these a try because they can be put right into the pot your plant or flowers are in. The design was cool too. I caught a few fungus gnats, but these things performed nowhere near the other brand I used in the past. Most gnats did not care that the sticky tapes were standing right there in the plant. The catchability is not very good. I went back to my other brand and resolved the fungus gnat issue.

reviewTitle: They work, but they are not sturdy
reviewDescription: These sticky traps are working - I have lots of fungus gnats trapped on them after only using them for a week. So that's good. But they are flimsy - I had trouble getting them into the soil around my houseplants, and some of them bent when I tried to insert them. I had to poke a hole with a fork and then insert. If you are willing to do that, they work and they're inexpensive.
The first time I purchased this product it worked fine but this second time the gnats do not even stick to the stickers.

I've got some little gnat-like things hanging around some plants in my home office, which obviously then fly around and annoy me while I'm working. I hoped these would make a significant dent in the issue. After about a week or so, no so much. One next to each plant, and I've captured a grand total of 8 insects. Thought it was maybe just bad luck, then I saw a but walk up from the dirt, halfway up the trap, then back down again. Then flying off. Very little dent has been made into the inspect population.

reviewTitle: Not so great for fruit flies.
reviewDescription: Since it's tomato season, and our harvest is often sitting on the counter, we've started to experience gnat problems. I broke out these traps that that we got earlier this year, and sat on flat across the top of a jar my wife has sourdough starter in that seems to draw the gnats like crazy. They can't get through the coffee filter over the top, so they just land on the filter. Since I placed it four days ago, I've caught a total of four gnats. The apple cider vinegar/soap cup has caught dozens. The adhesive on this is not sufficient for fruit flies. I've seen where they land on the trap and end up flying off later. The four i've caught is because I made a point of pushing them down while they were on the trap. I even saw one fruit fly walking across the sticky part. You could tell he was getting slightly stuck, but it was movement like if someone was walking through deep mud. Kind of a jerky pace across the surface.

RE "...60 Pieces Classic Sticky Traps for Fungus Gnat (Plants), Fruit Fly, Whitefly, and Fly | Yellow Sticky Insect Catcher Traps for Indoor/Outdoor, Kitchen, and Plants | Non-Toxic, 4 Shapes"... by GreenKeeper. Short Review : These don't work. I don't know what the other reviewers are talking about regarding these sticky flying insect traps working so well for fruit flies. They really don't. Not at all. Long Review : I have a new food dehydrator. Being a newbie, I didn't realize that the dehydration/drying time would be so lengthy. I purchased a jumbo amount of organic apples and kept them in the refrigerator in bags (until I could process, slice, and start drying them). I saw one or two apple fruit flies in the fridge (they seemed to like it in there)? A couple/few flew out (some stayed) when the refrigerator doors were opened. This was about 10 days ago. I saw no more than 5 - 10 fruit flies in the kitchen, flying around. A couple hung out by the humidifier, and one loitered by an unscented package of baby wipes...that's it! They weren't by the kitchen sink drain (sealed with a stopper) or trash bin (also sealed). So, I wasn't too concerned. Then they multiplied. I used the DIY method : Apple Cider Vinegar + dish soap, which worked, but within 5 hours, I was shocked to see that I had snared over 200+ fruit flies! Seeing 5 - 10 and snaring over 200+ freaked me out. Fruit flies can't bite, but I started feeling itchy just knowing they were around. Notable : No one blogging/vlogging or commenting on the r/insect or r/dehydrating Reddit subs mentioned that I needed to throw the apples away. So, I'm embarrassed to say that I didn't toss them at first (I have, now). These yellow sticky traps were inexpensive and mentioned "fruit fly control" in the title and description, but more importantly, the RAVE reviews on Amazon convinced me that they must be amazing. I don't get it, reviewers. Maybe these low-tech sticky cut-outs are snaring other flying insects (doubtful), but they definitely do NOT work for fruit flies. The other options (eg pricey plug-ins, low-cost apple + pineapple-shaped catchers, super-cheap sticky paper streamer-things) are unnecessary (imo). If you have fruit flies – TOSS THE FRUIT. Toss everything the fruit was in. Clean all surfaces. Set out low, shallow bowls with ACV + a couple of drops of dish soap. Don't bother with the poking holes in cling-wrap-thing because it doesn't work. Don't use tall glasses or taller Mason jars - the fruit flies will just hang out on the edge of the jar and won't dive in (it's possible they know it's a trap). These sticky yellow things don't work. Don't bother. PS Amazon's Ai "Rufus" says : Fruit flies can reproduce very quickly, with females able to lay hundreds of eggs in their short lifespan. Egg to Adult : - A female fruit fly lays eggs on fermenting food or in drains. - Eggs hatch into larvae (maggots) within 24–30 hours. - Larvae feed on the food (and molt). - Larvae move to a dry surface and turn into pupae. - Adult fruit flies emerge after a few days. - Adult fruit flies are able to reproduce and begin metamorphosis within two days. ⏱ Time is of the essence.

I even put a tad bit of honey on bottom of one still nothing sat a glass of red wine next to a few and put one ontop of the glass still nothing sat 1 flying pest has stuck to it they are driving me nuts. I put a banana out and 1 on top the banana holder still nothing no stuck fruit fly either ! Did not work at all for myself !

So many positive reviews! Kind of scratching my head here. I am currently standing in my kitchen watching fruit flies land on these things, walk around on them just to prove that they can, and then fly off. They yell: "Smell ya later!" in their tiny little voices, and then fly right in my face. Yes, I removed the paper and exposed the "sticky" surface. Don't even go there. And it's not like they even have a preference for these "traps"... these things are just like any other inanimate object in my kitchen. Isn't a trap supposed to lure the pest onto or into the trap so that they can be murdered?? There doesn't seem to be any luring going on here. Certainly not any murder going on, sadly, since I paid nine bucks just so I could watch these flies getting murdered. Help me understand. Here's something interesting! While I'm standing in my kitchen utterly baffled over the 4.5 star average review of this product, I filled a stew pot with water and some dish soap and put it in the sink. I had burned some chili in it - probably because I was distracted by being so angry at fruit flies - and it needed to soak for awhile. When I went back ten minutes later, there were eight dead fruit flies trapped in the soap bubbles. There were zero fruit flies stuck to the traps. Do the math.

reviewTitle: These sticky traps are a trap: it is torture to try to use them.
reviewDescription: The problem with these particular sticky traps is that the wax paper is difficult to remove. Once you have finally removed all the wax paper on one side and you try to remove the wax paper from the top or bottom of the other side, there is no way to hold the item without your fingers sticking to the trap. The wax paper thus is poorly designed: it adheres too much to the sticky trap and I could not get it off them. I had to use two of them with the wax paper still on one side of the bottom, and after working on two of them I gave up and am throwing them away. I never had this problem with other brands of sticky trap.

reviewTitle: Trap Doesn't Attract Flies, Spills, Improvement required - Trap Parts Potentially Useful for DIY
reviewDescription: Trap Doesn't Catch Flies & Spills Unavoidably! Have been using for over a month - haven't caught a single fly (but have other traps that catch smaller flies just fine). This Trap is really no different from small vinegar based fly traps, but is intended to catch larger flies. The flies are supposed to enter the top grid (should be attracted to the weak detergent mixture inside as it smells like weak vinegar which they like), and are supposed to fall into the dilute detergent mixture in the packet sitting inside, which would kill them. But not only did flies seem to be completely not attracted, unfortunately, but the packets are shaped such that you can't attach or remove the grid/lid, without one side of the detergent packet snaps on the legs of the lid, and spills as you open it. So you end up with much less liquid in the trap, which will dry out quickly. I will be trying out my own apple cider vinegar solutions to try to improve the attraction rate, and possibly will add a small amount of detergent, though that may not be required (will require testing), as there seems to be a real gap in the market for a small non-toxic liquid based trap like this, for larger flies (there are plenty of effective vinegar traps for the small flies already). These traps will be worthwhile if I can at lease reuse the casings ... but not sure I can even get that to work yet.

reviewTitle: DO NOT use in areas where people will be for an extended period of time""
reviewDescription: "You could mention easy to use, value for money or longevity" -- Amazon's suggestion for this product review Per the back of the product package: "If this product is used within homes, use only in closets, wardrobes and cupboards." More generally, it can be used in "garbage rooms, lavatories, floor drains (to sewer), entries and vestibules, offices, locker rooms, machine rooms, boiler rooms, utility equipment, garages, mop closets and product storage (after canning and bottling)." "This product may be used in non-food/feed areas... DO NOT use in areas where people will be for an extended period of time." Seems like it's probably not a good idea to use this product in the home. What if I go for broke and put it next to a window that I like to keep open at least sometimes? "Place... away from windows.... [as] drafts, weather and other conditions may affect performance." The above quotes are from the back of the package. As for the novella we are given to read on this product's Amazon page, buried near the end is this handy little warning: this product should not be used in areas where people spend more than 15 hours per day. You mean, areas like my freaking HOME?? That's a deceptively important warning that I totally failed to notice before buying this product. The active ingredient in this product is an insecticide called dichlorvos. According to Wikipedia, dichlorvos is a neurotoxin that also damages insect DNA. That seems to be how it kills insects, but it has been shown to affect higher-order animals, as well, including humans. "Acute and prolonged exposure may lead to death, genotoxic, neurological, reproductive, carcinogenic, immunological, hepatic, renal, respiratory, metabolic, dermal and other systemic effects. Its toxicity is due to the ability of the compound to inhibit acetyl cholinesterase at cholinergic junction of the nervous system." ("Dichlorvos toxicity: A public health perspective", Henshaw Uchechi Okoroiwu and Iwara Arikpo Iwara). Prevalent in urban waterways, each tenfold increase in organophosphate metabolites (like dichlorvos) is associated with a 50-70% increase in the odds of ADHD in children. (Wikipedia) The DHHS says it may reasonably be anticipated to be a carcinogen. The EPA has revised the acceptable use parameters for dichlorvos down multiple times, and the EU has banned it outright. The Washington State Department of Health issued a warning about the chemical called "Pest Strips with Dichlorvos." They wrote, "Organophosphate pest strips (strips that contain dichlorvos) are legal, but we are concerned they are making people sick, especially if they are not used according to their directions. We are working... to improve warnings... and make the restrictions about occupied areas more clear on the product label. The strips should not be used in general living or sleeping areas. "The label also says the pest strips can be used in vacation homes, cabins, and mobile homes - but only if “unoccupied for more than four months immediately following placement of pest strip."" (end quote) I have two young children in my home. Thank goodness that I am assiduous about reading the instructions that manufacturers provide on their products, or I would have exposed my family to a carcinogenic neurotoxin that was never supposed to be in home in the first place. And I'm not the only one. Reading the Q&A's, it's evident that many people who have bought this product are completely oblivious to its risks, meaning they did NOT read the manufacturer's instructions. For example, somebody (apparently trying to piece together how a product with so many restrictions could possibly be meant for home use) asked if it would be effective throughout the home if placed in a closet. One after another, other customers responded, "Why would you do that? I put it here and there and everywhere in my home! It's great!" I can't blame them. We've gotten used to a corporate world that looks out for our safety -- if only at the behest of the federal government. Like others, I bought this product assuming it was safe for my home simply because it's on Amazon. I assumed, without even thinking about it, that if a commercial/industrial-use product with only a handful of safe residential applications were to be sold on Amazon, this would be made very clear in the product title alone, not to mention the subsequent text and images. Other sellers are careful not to mislead and disappoint buyers about much less serious things. We should all know better than to totally cede all responsibility for our own health and welfare to regulators and businesses. I hope somebody reads this and buys something else.

The active chemical in this product has not been used in US for a long time and for good reason. I did not do enough research on MSDS prior to purchasing. That's on me. Not to mention the fact it is marketed to kill Fruit Flys, which it does, however most fruit flys are found in Kitchens and Bathrooms... The product clearly states do not use in areas where people are likely to frequent, and to use in closets, or other confined areas where you will NOT FIND FRUIT FLYS! This product is falsely marketed as being safe, and can cause harm to you and your family/pets if used improperly. For those who wrote positive reviews that used this Chemical in their Kitchen, I am shocked at the fact you would even consider using such a nasty chemical in an area where you prepare food for your family and your family goes to get drinks.

I bought these traps to put in my kitchen and bathrooms. In our kitchen, we tend to get fruit flies around our coffee machine. In the bathroom, we get them around the sinks, as they come out of the overflow drains and like to hang around my potted plants. These come in a 6-pack, which includes 6 small plastic cases and 6 single-use solution tubs. To setup, you take on plastic case, and one solution tub. Peel the sealed cover off of the solution tub, drop it into the case, and put the lid on the case. From there, you just place it in the area where you notice flying insects. The solution looks unremarkable, visually, but smells slightly of vinegar. After these had dried up, it looked somewhat goopy, but still smelled of vinegar. Putting a bit of water into the solution tub quickly reconstitutes the solution. Given this, I believe the solution is nothing more than vinegar, water, and soap, which is the recipe for a DIY fruit fly trap, but in a MUCH smaller scale. The first night I put these out, I caught a single fruit fly in the bathroom, which made me very hopeful that these were to solution to my problem. Unfortunately, these seem to be absolutely ineffective, as that first fruit fly I caught has been THE ONLY fruit fly I’ve caught, even though you can see them flying around these things. I’m not sure if the solution is too weak, too small amount, or just not an effective solution in the first place. Given that I’ve had these in place for months at this point, and have only caught one single fly, which I believe was a complete fluke, I have to come to the conclusion that these are a complete sham that do absolutely nothing, which the company probably knows, but still decides to cash in on people needing a real solution. Overall, these are a major disappointment, and I would not recommend these to anyone.

Not at all effective. We have had an issue with tiny fruit flies. Needed an alternative to the plug-in for a length of time I was going away. After 2 weeks, undisturbed, not one insect collected. I had more in a small water cup-catch located in yet a different area, but zero in the trap. I returned the plug in, and overnight had at least 10 on the sticky paper catch. Don’t bother with this product.

my house was randomly full of flies one day so I ordered this. Not even one fly showed any interest at all. Also, the product has to be assembled by peeling off the lid and then putting another lid on top, but it’s all the way full of the insect poison and it’s way too easy to spill

reviewTitle: Please use something that REALLY works! Save yourself frustration and further annoyance!
reviewDescription: Due to missing garbage day, my cans outside (which are very close to my backdoor) started growing a colony of flies. Some winged adventurers managed to get into my home because I cannot hermetically seal it. Anyway, I tried everything to get rid of them humanely. I bought this in a last ditch effort. Do NOT waste your money. These may attract fruit flies but NOT houseflies. It did not get rid of even 1 fly out of 50ish flies. The clear sticky sheets worked well. Stick with those! (Pun intended!) Check the pic. The sticky clear sheets were not noticed until they were already stuck. They literally died next to the Hot Shot Flying Insect Trap as opposed to going inside and getting trapped. This product is messy. Just removing the seal causes the liquid to spill. I am removing the product immediately to the trash. I doubt their claims of it being pet safe are any more legitimate than their claims that it traps flies. BUYER BEWARE!!!!!

I would give these 0 stars if I could. I’ve been battling fruit flies in my kitchen for weeks and was desperate. I hoped the reviews were wrong, and these seemed so easy to use. 1) They’re not easy to use. When you open the foil in the package, it’s almost impossible not to spill the liquid on yourself. They are filled the top. 2) I have lots of fruit flies and other flies (we live in the south and keep our back door open with a sheer curtain so our dogs can go in and out) and not a single fly has entered this trap. I’d have been better off with cling wrap over a cup filled with vinegar. Save your money and don’t make the same mistake as me. Only reason they get a single star might be for aesthetics. They look nice on the counter, that’s it.

reviewTitle: SAVE YOUR $$ TRY SOMETHING MORE EFFECTIVE
reviewDescription: SAVE....YOUR....MONEYYYY. These are no more effective than putting out a bowl of dishwater or a sweet liquid. I have many plants in my house thus have gnats. At the same time I purchased these I also purchased clear see-through window gnat traps that you stick to your window. I put 3 window traps on a window and they each caught approx 200 gnats each. And my Hot-Shot traps? One trap caught 0 gnats, another caught 1 gnat, and another caught approx 20 so these traps are no more effective than putting out a saucer of water. Save....your...money. If i relied upon the Hot-Shot traps alone I wouldn't have a full undestanding of my overall gnat problem.

reviewTitle: No Flies Were Harmed in the Using of this Item!
reviewDescription: Not one fly or fruit fly had any interest in the solution. I purchased these to replace the containers I generally use to kill fruit flies because the description claimed it would kill house flies as well. I left a few around the house for several weeks and instead of the ten to twenty fruit flies I usually get in my home-made solution, I caught nothing. Not one house fly or fruit fly. I can hear the flies all laughing at me for spending $12 on an attractive box of water. As an experiment I put my usual solution in the containers and within a few days I had several dead fruit flies, although no house flies. I will keep the containers because they are more attractive than the clear containers I generally use, but I will just add ACV & a touch of dish soap instead of buying the replacement pods.

reviewTitle: Not a single insect was trapped
reviewDescription: After receiving these from Amazon, we immediately opened up three of them and placed them in various locations in the kitchen and living room. Particularly, we placed these near spots where we've seen mosquitos, flies, and gnats. Sadly, none of these traps were able to get a single insect after three weeks of sitting out. The liquid eventually dried up and we just threw the container away (empty). Just a waste of money for us. We ended up buying an electric bug zapper that gets the job done.

These are useless. The flying insects don't even go inside. Ease of use, Opening it is messy. The super stuck covering is a thin foil thing that has alittle area you have to struggle with to grab, then sliding it off is difficult and makes the fluid inside splash out. The first one went from opening it to the trash because by the time I got the darn thing open there was hardly any solution left. I put them out around the house. some of them were not steady to sit upright, thus fell over, liquid spilled out easily. The others that did stay seated, never had any bugs fly/crawl into them. They would land on top but fly off. I have had them sitting out for a month, they are empty of fluid and bugs.
If left sitting around, even for a month, these things seem to lose their stickiness. I watched gnats walk around on the traps without getting trapped.

I bought these sticky traps because of little black bugs flying around in the house. We have many plants in the kitchen and the bugs were coming from one of the plants. First, the instructions say not to touch the traps directly. So, I put gloves on. The traps ended up sticking to the gloves and almost tore them. By the time I got the traps off the gloves and into the plants, there was a ton of dirt stuck to the traps. After putting all of the traps in the plants, I waited. Days later I checked and there were NO bugs stuck to the traps. In fact I saw a bug flying around instead of being stuck to the trap. Needless to say these traps do not work at all. I would NOT recommend them.
Waste of money

reviewTitle: huge waste of money, they do nothing to attract bugs.
reviewDescription: I have a fly, gnat and any other kind of flying insect problem but these do nothing to actually attract them to the film. These are peel and stick and i'll admit the adhesive would hold up with bugs, they unless you are in a 2x2x2 room with no vents/windows good luck getting them to actually stick. They have to have a reason to land on them, and they don't. there is no chemical or odor that attracts them so you are just paying for glue and plastic which is a waste. I have like 30 of these things and nothing to do with them. didn't want to go through the hassle of a return, waste of my time.

reviewTitle: These do not work at all
reviewDescription: These are ridiculous. My neighbor and I both had terrible fruit type flies in our homes this year. I bought these out of sheer desperation to be rid of the gnat sized nuisances and gave some to him as well. Not one fly caught between two separate houses in need of some fly catching. A neighbor three doors down used the old fashioned awful hanging film things and was constantly replacing them as they were covered in stuck insects. I wasn’t that desperate, but, don’t buy these. They absolutely don’t work as advertised which frankly sounded logically feasible. Nope

reviewTitle: 4th day in use and it has caught ZERO bugs!
reviewDescription: What a waste of money. After 4 days It has caught nothing NOTHING! At first, gnats or whatever they were hovered around it and I was impressed. I didn't realize I had so many of them flying around unseen. But then they just hovered in the vicinity of the trap and stayed there and didn't actually step into the actual sticky paper as though they knew this was a lure and they weren't buying it. Either the trap is a complete bust or the insects have become smarter. I can't believe I was lured into buying it. I was the dumb one.
Useless! They wasn’t even sticky when received. Waste of money.

I have an apartment full of gnat-like pests that are concentrated in my kitchen and bathroom (near my cat's box) and after 3+ days, none of the 5 'traps' has a single pest on it. I stuck them on the fridge, a wall and tiled walls in my bathroom. I also hung one. All of those I stuck on flat surfaces fell off within minutes. If it wouldn't be an inconvenience to return something that cost a little over $5, I would.

I got these 4 days ago and immediately put them all through my house. Gnats have been a real problem!! After 4 days I still have the same problem!! This product doesn’t attract the gnats at all. For me they’re a waste of time and I hate throwing my money out the window.

We recently had a gnat infestation in my house and was so excited when I came across this product and reviews. These did absolutely nothing. I watched as several landed on them and flew away like nothing. Such a bummer

I have purchased 3 of these units and at first I thought they worked very well. But over all three units have discolored and look burnt. I know some discoloration can happen over time, but this has also discolored a surge protector strip and I don't feel it is safe to use. I have been in contact with BugMd and they referred me back to Amazon. I cannot recommend and if you have them and are using them. Please check them periodically and watch for dark brown discoloration. I will attach images.

reviewTitle: Does not work for Gnats at all
reviewDescription: This does not work for gnats at all. A container with water works better for gnats. As long as gnats is in product description this product is false advertising.

This product does not seem to work on fruit flies. I have had it right over the kitchen compost bin for a few weeks now and have maybe four fruit flies in it. I do not recommend this product. The vinegar/Dawn bowl works much better.

Haven't caught a single gnat. I've been using the other brand which I have caught many but still am having a problem. So I thought I would try this one. This one the light is more brighter but as I said I haven't caught any with it.

Do not recommend the product. I have tried other brands of plug in “pest” and bug units, but this one is the worse. I fell for the idea of getting rid of the tiny gnatty bugs, but after several days of using this product, it has only got 3 little bugs. But I still have them all over the room!

reviewTitle: Don't waste your money on BugMD products.
reviewDescription: Don't waste your money. This is the second time I have made the mistake of buying something from BugMD. It will be the last time. I previously bought their ant control product. The only ants that died drowned. I recently bought this product. It arrived on Sunday and I immediately set it up. Two days later, about 14 flies or gnats have been caught in one trap, nothing in the second. At this rate they will die of old age rather than in the traps.
Flat doesn't do the job. After 2 months the pad only had four bugs, while the old fashioned fly paper 5 feet away had HUNDREDS of them..
Has not worked at all. It’s not attracting or catching anything. Do not recommend.

reviewTitle: I wish I could give zero stars
reviewDescription: Update: Total scam product if you want to catch flys. The system wouldn't let me return under the "item defective or doesn't work" section. I had to select "no longer needed". Dont waste your money on these. I have been so excited to try this to catch the flies that keep invading my home. Last night and today I have watched multiple flies fly around and never go into this light trap. Isn't working at all!! One fly just hung out on the wall a foot a way from the light. I will keep these a little longer to give a solid test before returning - but it looks like a product return is in my future.

I wish I could give it 5 stars, but sadly I can't. I have had it for 3 days and it has not attracted a single bug. I have a lot of gnats due to tomato season and was hoping this would do the trick. Not so. I will give it another week, but may return it. Disappointing. update: I gave this one star because it didn't work. I did give it extra time and after a couple of weeks it finally started catching gnats. But now I cannot find refills for it. Does anyone know where I can get them?
I did not like this product. It did not attract the bugs
It's not a very good product; it didn't catch tiny flying fruit flies, but it did catch gnats and tiny mosquito.

I have had this plugged in the kitchen for over 24 hours now, not one gnat stuck to the back. They have been flying all around it, attracted to the light, but haven’t caught one. I have more luck catching gnats with a bowl of vinegar and dish soap.
Not sure what type of gnat this is supposed to attract I have had this for about two weeks and caught 4 gnats while there is a swarm while trying to cook

Ok the very first BugMD kit I purchased worked—the entire sheet was covered in bugs within two weeks. I purchased this refill pack and have caught 2 bugs after a month and it’s peak bug season (spring—>summer).
Very easy to set-up and use. Set it next to the fruit bowl where the flies are abundant. After 3 days, only one fly in the trap.
I ordered it got it and tried to use it only to find there are no traps in it pretty useless until I get inserts
Works for fruit flies and gnats, but I was really trying to catch the hundreds of house flys that follow our dogs inside every summer. These haven't caught a single one.
I plugged this in and it seems to be catching gnats no problem but flies aren’t phased by it

reviewTitle: Worked good but only for 6 months
reviewDescription: I really liked this as it worked attracting and sticking flies, gnats, and other small flying insects. Used two stickers. However it started flickering on and off and died. There was a small slight light brown mark on the back as if it over heated. Only worked for 6 months.

Ordered three new zappers but only two were new one was used and it would not light up. I returned the item as it was DOA when arrived. The DOA product seems to be a used product sent to me as the package was opened and torn. As for the other two zappers it seems to be working some what. It zaps mosquitoes but not the flies. Using it in the back indoor porch
If I had known item only had a 30 day warranty, I would not have purchased it! Amazon should be more up front with that detail! Product performance was unsatisfactory--did not even last 1 year.

reviewTitle: Great but stopped working after 3 months…
reviewDescription: I’m giving this two stars because although it worked great and you could hear the bugs getting zapped hard, the light stopped working about 3 months later. I recently bought another one to see if it lasts longer. What I hate is that there is no On/Off button or switch. It’s only plug or unplug.

It worked until it didn’t! I used it inside my house and I felt good to hear it zap every now and again. But it’s been close to a year now and it finally went dead. I’ve heard of people having these for many years outside in the elements. Not sure how to rate this one. Two stars feels pretty fair. Perhaps you should look at a different brand.

It does kill gnats, but maybe not all gnats are actually attracted to the light or maybe it needs to be hung to the ceiling. I’m not sure, but it didn’t kill all of the gnats created by a trash bag that we forgot to throw out before going outta town.
I have on my room as recently i saw a lot of mosquitos unfortunetly im still getting bitten even im sleeping next to the lamp i hear every other time a bug getting zipped but not mosquitos

reviewTitle: Don’t expect it to last long
reviewDescription: 2 units died. Didn’t last 3 months. It did work well for the 3 months. So it is unfortunate about the short lifespan.

Initially the zapper worked great. But after less than a month it stopped working completely. It hung from me porch away from the rain, So, it never got wet. Im shocked and horribly dissapointed that this item didnt last longer, it didnt even last one summer. I guess I will have to buy another brang. At least the previous brand I bought lasted 4 years. I expected far better considering this brand used to mean quality and durability... I wont buy any of their products again;
Stopped working after 4 months of purchase! Specifically, it sparked and I quickly turned it off and it hasn't worked since.

reviewTitle: Stopped working after 4 months. Didn’t like writing this review.
reviewDescription: I bought it on 6/28, used it for my garage. It quit last week. Took the top cover off it has a burnt resistor. Doesn’t seem like am able to get hold of the seller. My last zapper lasted 15 years.

Not really attracting bugs and be careful if you get it on your skin or clothing! The traps were not wrapped in anything straight from the box so the sticky stuff got all over my hands. This stuff is very difficult to get off of your skin!

reviewTitle: Not mean for inside. Probably better outside
reviewDescription: These were not helpful at all. Also, the instructions said to add honey or syrup to it. Nope, not a single fly. This was returned. The only upside is that it doesn’t smell.

Have had both set up for about a week now with both of them having 0 fruit flies stuck to them… one we used honey and the other we used syrup. We have a few other traps that have been getting fruit flies as we currently are dealing with an infestation of them. Maybe they will work soon, but as of now they are both striking out. I like the build of them and the concept of how they function, just wish they worked.

While purchasing this item it states that you can order it as a 2 pack as the smallest amount which I thought was great but when it arrived there was only one trap in the box with clearly enough empty space in the box for a second trap. Unfortunate but oh well. The one trap that I received works well just wish I had gotten both of them especially for the amount they cost.

I purchased a box of 4 and deployed them in an apartment I was renting in the Dominican Republic for 1 month. It did not attract or snare a single fly, mosquito or moth. Perhaps the bugs over there are super smart or the product just is not that effective. I would not purchase this particular product again. Until I do, I'll just use a good ole fashioned fly swatter!

I have purchased these many times before. I just received my order and the Reservoir that holds the Honey is much smaller then before. It is harder to pour the Honey into this smaller tub. Not pleased!

reviewTitle: Not for use in Florida heat
reviewDescription: After 2 days hanging in the Florida heat, the top separated from the main body of the unit as the adhesive melted. This resulted in a sticky floor and a unit that only 2 days old, in the trash. Item was working but failed in the Florida heat. A pity. Back to fly tape!

reviewTitle: Do not work as good as othe ones from Amazon. I got a cheap attack. My loss
reviewDescription: Do not work even with honey in them

Would not purchase again if you touch it with your finger on the yellow part your finger has to be washed five or six times to get it off you also having a teenager put it up it got stuck to her t-shirt what a mess that was would not recommend

reviewTitle: Dangerously sticky, not worth the money
reviewDescription: Almost too sticky, somehow doesn’t attract anything. Had it up for 3 weeks and it only caught 2 flies, the rest just flew right past it. Also, these are so sticky you need to be REALLY careful when handling and choosing a spot to hang. Just get fly paper if you’re considering this product, it’ll be more effective and a better value.

We placed them all around, and we’re convinced that they are not at all attracted to these sticky things, but may happen to bump into them on the way out of the plant soil. Over several days, only a few gnats got stuck to them. But they continue to fly around the room until we smash them 1 by 1 with our hands. These “traps” are basically useless to us.

If you like having random yellow plastic shapes on your walls for positively no reason, this product is for you. If this isn’t your life’s goal, though, save yourself the frustration & pass on this scam of a product. We have about 13 of them up & in 3 days have caught…wait for it…ZERO of anything despite having a significant problem with tiny moths, fruit flies & mosquitos. We continue to get bitten all over because I got duped. I should’ve known better, but now YOU do!

reviewTitle: Fruit flies have *zero* interest in these
reviewDescription: We have a fruit fly problem in the break room at work. I put up half a dozen of these around the room, and throughout the course of the next week, not ONE single fruit fly was caught on any of them. I still had them buzzing all around me as if the traps weren't even there. The liquid apple cider vinegar-based traps work infinitely better--I caught several dozen with those the week prior. But those traps get knocked over and spilled too easily with the nincompoops I work with, so I was looking for a more stable solution--this is not it. Completely ineffective.
NOT WORTH GETTING THIS ITEM. THE ONLY IT CATCHES IS DIRT!!

These have a horrible sticky residue that sticks to you and everything else that comes in contact with it. Not just "sticky" like better products I've purchased have. It's some sort of glue substance...these are nightmare sticky! These went into the trash.
Ordered to try & get rid of the gnats around my house plants. Sadly, these didn't help at all. Thank goodness they were inexpensive.

I bought this based on the glowing recommendation on the "Pioneer Woman" (aka Ree Drummond) web site. I have gnats or fruit, I don't know the difference, when I first moved to Florida people told me they were call "no-see-ums" but I DO see-um around my sink and houseplants. These traps are absolutely worthless. Set up a few in the houseplants on my kitchen counter and living room window sill. 5 days later not one gnat caught.
No good.The small flys didn’t even stick to them .I witnessed this many times .Waist of money

I'm hating gnats right now so I ordered. With a clean kitchen, 8 traps, and hundreds of gnats. I've caught 1, while they line my empty trash can. I'm trying apple cider vinegar and dish soap next. It's the first thing recommended. I hate negative comments, but they said "for gnats, they love it". 1 grain of sugar in a drop of water attracts 50.

3 weeks and zero fruit flies or any kind of insects caught. Set up in 3 locations in the house and zilch. I should have read all the 1 star reviews but there were lots of good reviews too but for the life of me I can't understand why, unless it's a scam

I bought these for whiteflies in my herb garden. They are extremely sticky!!! I put four in yesterday and was hopeful they would take care of things. Earlier today it dawned on me how sticky they were may be hazardous to other creatures. So I went to check them out and to my utter dismay, I found FOUR baby lizards stuck to one. Completely helpless and not moving I ran inside with the trap. It took me 40 minutes to CAREFULLY remove each teeny tiny bit of them using oil and a pointed cotton swab. :( So far two perked up and I released them back into the garden, but the other two are a little worse for wear. I left them in a jug at home to recoup and I’m praying they are ok. There really should be a warning about the stickiness and other animals. Buyer beware!
I brought in some knats when I brought in my plants this fall so i put these in them. My knats must be smarter than average.

reviewTitle: They are okay until the wind blows and your plant get’s stuck to it
reviewDescription: Work as advertised

I did not like that they are flimsy and hard to get into the plant soil. Once you get them in there they are sticky. Be sure to place them before removing the outside backing or you will have a very hard time keeping them in place. Once I got them in they worked fine.... But I will not buy them again.

Eeek! Gnats in all my houseplants. These sticky traps definitely capture a lot of bugs though I'm not sure whether or not they are meant to totally eliminate the bugs, maybe just capture them. They are extremely sticky and you will capture a lot of bugs. Two things that didn't work well for me: The spike is difficult to push into the soil, it is too broad and not tapered enough. Tooo difficult to pull the paper off of the fancy shapes, I wish they had used a more basic shape like a rectangle or a circle.

I think the decorative traps are very nice looking. I am not sure how affective they are because I have only had them in my plants for about a week. I do see some dark specs on some of them. I will continue to watch and see how effective they are.

reviewTitle: These are good for some people I guess, but I won't order again
reviewDescription: I was excited to get these as it seemed an easier alternative to buying large sticky cards that I would cut into smaller strips. I also was interested in the ease of use of being able to stick them into the soil. However that is not possible. They are made of a very thin, very floppy plastic. Unless your soil is beyond loose, you will not be sticking these directly into the dirt. The maker seems to realize this and they give you a tiny toy plastic shovel to dig a hole for them. I do not want nor did I intend on having to dig a hole in my root zone to put these into the dirt. Overall, I am not pleased with this product and will not order it again. I gave it 3 stars because they are yellow and are sticky but there are far superior products on the market. Not recommended.

reviewTitle: Not the fix I was looking for.
reviewDescription: This product may work well for some people but, I haven't experienced that luck yet. I have managed to catch a few fruit flies here and there. However, after watching a fruit fly land on the sticky surface, walk around and then fly away, I was a bit disappointed. *Note: I added a star only because of have a friend who highly recommended the product to me after indicating they work great and the sticky surface on the ones that she had were completely covered with fruit flies.

reviewTitle: They are sticky, but they don't attract mosquitos
reviewDescription: These are sticky enough, and I didn't have trouble using them, but I have some mosquitos that get in my bathroom door from outside, and they have persisted for weeks, because stuck in a plant pot, the product doesn't attract mosquitos. My bug count is 2 unfortunate gnats in 3 weeks of use.

The little flying insects are not attracted to them and they do not stick to the trap, either. After approximately 4 days with several flying insects around... the 10 traps that we put out have attracted exactly one bug... I think it was on accident.
These are the only sticky traps I've ever used which catch NOTHING! Bugs walk right over them.

reviewTitle: Cute idea but Awful to handle for use.
reviewDescription: I was extremely disappointed. Even with nail polish remover it is difficult getting the sticky junk off my fingers, which is impossible to avoid. I haven't looked yet to see if I can send them back I highly doubt it. I had purchased a different version from a local store and should have looked for refills for that. They were very easy to use.

We have been fighting an infestation of fungal gnats.. I was given traps made by a different brand that have been working. Needed more and bought these. Put them side by side. 24 hours later, this brand caught 0. Previous brand has bunches front and back. These do not work for fungal gnats.
No good

They were super easy to hang and put in pots a different things. We somehow got gnats in the house someway, but my teen son does it in his room and I did have them downstairs in the kitchen mainly and then I will looked in his room, and sure enough, they were in his room so bad so that is a problem obviously with him eating in there, but the sticky things did not help for that problem with gnats. I woke up and looked and not one was on those sticky.
do not waste your money. did not work at all.
Did not work

These sticky traps are not effective. These have been placed around the house, specifically in the kitchen and near the trash can in an attempt to reduce gnat infestations, but have not been successful. There is a possibility that they will work outdoors, but not indoors.

I wanted something to catch the tiny white flies, but when I saw a ladybug dead in the trap, I immediately threw them away. These beneficial insects are too important in my garden to destroy a single one. I decided to become friends with the white flies.

I've done everything I can to get fruit flies to land on them. They're after my beer so I placed one on top of three empty beer cans and poured a spot of beer on the center of the trap. The flies will land all over the beer cans below but won't even go for the beer poured onto the sticky surface. It's as if they're intentionally avoiding it. Waste of money.

Had a small fungus gnat infestation & after reading reviews decided to give these a shot. Set out 5 & the gnats just walked all over them without sticking (yes, I peeled off the white protective paper) only a couple of gnats actually got stuck. Returning the remainder. Very disappointed.

reviewTitle: Doesn't work to trap flying insects like mosquitos or gnats
reviewDescription: We placed these at about a dozen locations inside our house, because we keep getting bit by mosquitos. They've been there for over a week. They've trapped absolutely no bugs of any kind, and we're still getting bit by mosquitos. As a test, I placed two sticky traps outside on a ceiling fan chain and on a patio table, where I know there are mosquitos, and only 1 mosquito was trapped after several days. Don't waste your money.

reviewTitle: Horrible do not buy. Did not get even one fruit fly
reviewDescription: Odd shape made to put into plant soil as pictured Tried to prop up on cabinet pulls and lean against appliances. Sticky both sides but was afraid to stick on counter in case left marks. Did not get one bug in 8 hours My partner spilled a few drops of orange juice a few inches from one of these and the OJ got more bugs than any of these. Reminder to me and you. Save the package these kind od long products come in and ask for refund from Amazon

reviewTitle: DO NOT BUY. Waste of money.
reviewDescription: These things do not work. The sticky fly trap caught ONE nat from house plants in 3+ weeks. I tested them in comparison to a different brand which caught 30+ nats in a week. I then added a tiny bowl of apple cider vinegar w a drop of dish soap next to the fly trap. Just overnight 4 nats drowned in the vinegar. The fly traps are completely worthless!
Doesn’t seem to attract fruit flies. Have had it in my plants for 2 months with no flies stuck on the product. Would NOT recommend.

These don't work at all! They're entirely useless. While a giant swarm of fruit flies buzzed around in my kitchen garbage can, and two of these sticky devices were attached to the sides of the can, only one fly was caught. ONE! Out of at least a hundred!!! They also haven't attracted flies along a countertop and in a window. What a waste of money! How very annoying!
This product is not worth buying.

Appears to have no attractive properties, relying on pure happenstance the insect may land on it. I've deployed 18 throughout the main living floor and despite seeing several gnats, zero have landed on any of the devices. Not impressed. Also, the butterfly design is difficult to remove the protective paper properly as it tears right up the middle. The shape of the others made for easy, one-piece removal. This one, not so much. Would not buy again nor recommend it to others.

I wanted to believe the positive reviews, but apparently I should have believed the negative ones. These things are not nearly sticky enough. Within about a minute of "installing," I saw a gnat literally land on the "sticky" surface, walk around a bit, and fly away. I left them out for a few more days, within inches of my plants that have MANY gnats, and didn't catch a single one.

I have a gnat issue in my apartment (like most apartments do in the summer lol) and I’ve put these in all my potted plants and all around the kitchen sink and not a single gnat has been trapped on these… it’s been a week and they all look spotless and I did peel them and everything! ):
Disappointed
it does work but takes longer than I thought
Only had it for a few weeks seems to be working okay but not much better than the other brands I have
Wouldn't buy again, for the price was expecting them to work much better
I've only had it for a few days and it seems to be working.

Over the summer we had a really big problem with fruit flies taking over our kitchen sink. I was trying all the home remedies we could do. Nothing was working. I bought this bug light with high hopes but it didn’t work as well as I thought it would. I didn’t want to spend the money on the Zevo so I went with the bigmd. Don’t get me wrong it did work but I also had to continue with other home remedies to get the fruit flies gone. It definitely is a good buy if you don’t want to spend the money on a Zevo just expect it to take a little while before the bugs are all gone.

reviewTitle: I did not read the fine print enough.

reviewTitle: Works for gnats and fruit flies
reviewDescription: This worked well for me for gnats and fruit flies. This product did not catch any flies, but I don’t have a big problem with flies anyway. It is liquid and is messy if knocked over.

We tend to get gnats and fruit flies around the kitchen sink, our countertop compost bin, and fruit basket, and we have used a variety of fly traps to control them. I was excited to try these Hot Shot Flying Insect Traps, but so far, I am not seeing a positive effect. First, I was a little concerned about the trap itself. The trap contains a liquid that easily spills, so you have to be careful to place it where it isn't at risk of being knocked over (away from kids and kitties, for example). I placed on of these traps on our kitchen countertop behind the sink and next to prime gnat real estate over ten days ago, but so far, it hasn't caught a single critter. This morning I saw a gnat hovering close to it and thought, "ok, today is the day the fly trap will do its thing." But I checked a few hours later, and still--not a single captured critter. This trap only has a couple more days to perform (the directions say to replace them every two weeks), but I am not too optimistic that I will suddenly see a different outcome from what I've experienced to date. If that changes, I will update my review. But as things stand today, the product is simply not performing as I'd expected.

Its is confusing. They are still around though. Id say it is meh, for me. Perhaps others will find better luck but Im going back to zapping. Perhaps its better for non kitchen rooms

So I have mixed feelings on this… the good: crazy sticky adhesive area, soft and not blinding light, the adjustable plug that allows you to install it in outlets that run both horizontally and vertically, quick set up, and safe for my pets and kids with no harsh chemicals or zaps of electricity. I also really appreciate the additional pack of sticky pads that comes with it - there’s nothing worse than getting a great product that has a replaceable element and then discovering that you’ve got to shell out more money if you want to continue to use it down the road. The bad: it’s kind of a statement piece- it’s pretty big, and that mixed with the purple light does make it a bit of an eyesore and very noticeable as soon as you walk in the room. I’m also not sure how well the purple light works, because the 3 fruit flies who have decided to invade my kitchen still haven’t gotten themselves stuck to the pad yet. I was expecting it to work a little quicker and for the little buggers to be firmly attached to the sticky part by now, but I haven’t given up hope that one day soon I’ll be greeted by beautiful silence instead of a slight buzz when I walk into my kitchen.

reviewTitle: Effective Fly Trap, but Packaging Needs Improvement
reviewDescription: The hanging fly trap does a decent job of catching flies, but my experience was mixed. The trap uses a glue-like substance that effectively attracts and captures flies. Once the trap is set up, it does its job well, significantly reducing the number of flies in the area. I noticed a difference within a day or two, and it’s satisfying to see the traps doing their work. However, the reason for my 3-star rating is the packaging. When I opened the box, one of the traps wasn’t packaged properly. The protective covering had come loose, causing the sticky glue to get all over my hand. Cleaning it off was a nightmare—the glue is incredibly tacky and difficult to remove. I had to use several different cleaning methods, and even then, it took a lot of scrubbing to get my hands clean. For a product that’s meant to be easy and hassle-free, this was a major downside. The traps work as advertised, but the packaging issue made the experience frustrating. If the manufacturer can improve the packaging to prevent this kind of mess, I’d consider giving it a higher rating. Until then, I’m hesitant to purchase it again despite its effectiveness.

reviewTitle: Stopped working; *WARRANTY* promise but NO! support channel
reviewDescription: I purchased the FVOAI Fruit Fly Trap in March 2025. It worked at first, then sat powered off for about four months. Today did not turn on. After checking with its own, I tried multiple USB adapters and cables; pressing the power button does nothing—no lights, no response. The bigger issue is support. The email listed on the brand’s site (contact at thefvoai dot com ) bounced (Gmail delivery failure), and the phone number on their site did not connect to a working support line. There was no support contact in the Amazon listing or inside the box brochure—even though the brochure promises: “If you Experience ny Issues with your device this (one yera period) we will be more than happy to replace it free of charge.” I had to hunt down their website just to try contacting them—and even that didn’t work. I understand products can fail. But a warranty is only as good as the ability to reach the company. I’ve wasted time troubleshooting and chasing dead contacts. If FVOAI provides a real channel and resolves this, I will update. Until then, consider the risk that you may be on your own if something goes wrong.

reviewTitle: This went from a 5-Star to a 1-Star... review updated
reviewDescription: When I first received this, I was truly amazed! (Original review below) But today - 31 days after purchasing - my review has gone from a 5-Star to a 4-Star. It lasted 30 days and on day 31 it died! After recommending this product to all my friends, I am now telling them to avoid it. The gnats were out of control this season—absolutely driving me crazy. In a moment of desperation, I took a chance and ordered this Star Trek looking trap. I plugged it in and set it on my desk, hoping for the best. A few hours later… nothing. The trap looked completely empty. I’ll admit—I was skeptical. But then this morning, I sat down at my desk and realized something amazing: I was gnat-free. I opened the trap and couldn’t believe my eyes—tons of those annoying little pests were stuck to the bottom! This thing works. It may not look like much at first, but give it a night and let it do its thing. I highly recommend it to anyone battling gnats, mosquitoes, or their sanity. P.S. It works but it doesnt get them all. the sticky pads are plenty sticky but the fan just isnt strong enough to pull some flies in. itll get the teeny ones but it wont pull in say a mosquito or a large fruit fly even. also the fan makes an unpleasant buzzing noise, so i wouldnt put it on a nightstand, maybe across the room. reviewTitle: Good design and style but motor is too noisy
reviewDescription: Cosmetically this is a nice unit for indoors. Catches good amount of insects. We got it for mosquitoes and gnats. Functionally works great. Too bad it makes so much noise. This is my second one. same noise -2 stars . I would think a single fan could run a lot quieter like in a PC computer. The concept and design are solid good, but the light machine motor noise is annoying.. if the company could quiet down the motor noise this would be a 5 star and worthy of inside the room close to the bed at night.

reviewTitle: Probably a good device for some, but utter failure for our particular flies.
reviewDescription: I really wanted this to work. We have a legit fly issue seasonally, where we are swatting like 20 a day for nearly a month. We tried to make it work, several location, but we watched flies zip all around it without going in. Literally NONE went it! Maybe because we are in Colorado at altitude? Whatever the case, they simply ignored it... kind of amazingly well. Anyways, it's well built, fairly quiet, looks good. It would be 5 stars if the flies actually went in. After a few weeks with nothing on the sticky pad we returned it.

reviewTitle: Absolutely worth it. - EDIT: Reduced stars for low longevity
reviewDescription: Edit: I'm still sold on the functionality of these things. The design is absolutely AWESOME! But the clear plastic ring that holds the LEDs gets cloudy and opaque after a while, and stops attracting the bugs. I'm super disappointed. I took one apart earlier today to see if there was anything I could do about it, but everything I did only made it worse. I might buy a 3D printer just so I can make some new ones because the design REALLY rocks on this thing when the LEDs aren't blocked by the bad choice in plastic. Side note: They're not really meant to be user serviceable, so I wouldn't expect anyone to be making replacement parts. If they could just make that white plastic ring out of something that doesn't get cloudy over time - I think they'd crush all their competition. Original review below for posterity. I got this and another of a different style at the same time. The other one is nowhere near as good as this. In just two days this thing caught all the stuff in the picture. I legit didn't even know I had full size goes flies in the house. The other style only had 2 fruit flies after 3 days. I highly recommend this style, as it's proved highly effective. Definitely more bang for your buck. In addition to the flies, and fruit flies this thing also catches moths. Absolutely recommend getting this, or one just like it.

For the most part this trap works as promised. The light is good and bright and it changes colors as indicated. The fan inside is pretty strong, I can feel it if I put my hand up to it. It does make a buzzing noise but it is not intrusive, we have every fan in the house going at all times so it just joins the background noise. The sticky pads are super sticky and I like that is fairly compact. On the first day it caught about 15 fruit flies day two it cought another 15-20. That wasn't quite fast enough for me since we had thousands in our kitchen so I added a bit of sourdough starter to the middle of the sticky pad to attract them and now it catches about 50 per day. This alone isn't going to get rid of your gnats if you are in the middle if an infestation. It is a good aid but you should pour bleach in your faucets and put treatment in your plant soil also.

reviewTitle: Didn't Like the Hum It Made
reviewDescription: Attractive design like a piece of decorative art. Very bright blue light. I did not like the hum it gave off from the fan inside. I'm very sensitive to background noise. It did a good job of attracting bugs, but I wound up giving the unit and replacement pads to my neighbor who is very thrilled with it.
It dose get some

I expected something that worked a bit better. It does work better at night when the insect can see the luring light but in the day not so much. Easy set up and it is quiet. It’s bigger than I expected as well. It’s bigger than a counter can opener. I don’t think the value for the money is there.

reviewTitle: Sucks, but not in a good way.
reviewDescription: This thing kinda sucks, but not in a good way. The suction isn't strong enough to suck them down to the sticky pad. They only get stuck because they go on the sticky pad themselves. I've watched gnats fly in and out of this machine with no issues.

Listed as a mosquito trap. Zero mosquitos. Yes, it will catch a couple of fruit flies but so will a cup of apple cider vinegar with clear plastic wrap and a few holes poked in it, and much cheaper at that. Save your money. Just a stupid gadget. Very disappointed. Two stars for the few fruit flies it did catch.

reviewTitle: Doesn't really work for fruit flies
reviewDescription: Every year we have a fruit fly issue at our cottage in Northern MI. There are many orchards and vineyards in the area and no matter how careful we are, we get some fruit flies each season. This year we happened to be at the cottage when we had a big outbreak. After a week of temps in the mid 80s, we had a weather shift, dropping the overnight temps into the low 60s with very little wind. The next morning fruit does were everywhere, presumably drawn to the heat of the house. They were outside, inside, everywhere. We rent this cottage and the UV trap had already been use for two weeks before we arrived. There were a few random insects but nothing terribly impressive. When the fruit flies hit, I put out several traditional bait traps. The photos show the difference. At the time of the photos, the UV trap had been in steady operation for 3 weeks. The red/ fruit trap was only out for 2 hours!!! I'm not kidding and not exaggerating. This is two hours. There are two issues here. (1) Fruit flies don't seem particularly attracted to UV light and (2) the suction of the fan isn't remotely adequate.

reviewTitle: Noisy after a few months of usage
reviewDescription: We really enjoyed using our FVOAI insect trap since we live in a humid city apartment that attracts many gnats and fruit flies. The trap worked well and did the job. However, after only a few months of usage, the quiet humming noise has now become much louder and the entire trap vibrates, making it difficult to have in a room without causing disruption. We are unsure what caused it to be this way since we only ever turn it on/off and leave it in one place. It’s too noisy to use. I tried searching for a way to contact FVOAI through Amazon and their website to see if I could get it replaced, but it doesn’t look like there’s any way to do so after the initial 30-day window. Unfortunate since we really liked this product but now are unable to continue using it due to the sudden malfunction.
This was working fine for a while and catching a few gnats here and there (certainly not all though) but I’ve had to stop using it because it’s making a crazy sound. See video. Too late for a refund.

The blue light does not attract the fruit flies in. However if you hold it and chase them around in sucks them in pretty good, but that's where the positives end. The sticky paper on the bottom of the trap is not strong enough to trap the fruit flies. We tried using duct tape but believe it or not they also won't stick to the duck tape. Some do get stuck but its only two or three out of ten. The max run time is 12 hours. After 12 hours when it turns off any trapped fruit flies fly right back out and are back in the house. As soon as you turn it off they start flying back out. So you can't turn it off and try to release them outside. I don't understand how this item made it out of trail testing and into full production.

1 YEAR 2025 UPDATE: NONE of the 6 machines I bought work a year later. The lights and fans either do not activate at all, or only activate weakly while pressing the button. I still stand by the fact that they work very well, but don't expect it to work next year. Its a LOT of environmental and monetary waste for a product that plugs into an outlet that wont even turn on the next year. ORIGINAL 2024 REVIEW: Relatively quiet and super effective. I can't stand summer bugs that get inside and these traps attract and trap all sizes of flying pests. To boost the effectiveness of these traps even further I set a bottle cap full of apple cider in the center of the light ring and that makes them even better. Would be nice if there was a feature built in for this purpose. I liked the first one i got so much I bought like 5 more to cover most rooms and in the basement.
I have a small unit with a light and a sticky pad that far outshines this noisy thing!

It was not what I read did some research on this but when I got home it was not battery operated which I wanted and thought that was I getting the fan was very noisy. And the light lit up the whole room it was like at the carnival and I let do it thing the next day I didn’t catch one Nate or anything so it going back

I was hoping this item could replace the bug zapper that I had been using but unfortunately it doesn’t work as well as the zapper. In my opinion It’s definitely not worth the money.

reviewTitle: FVOAI Fruit Fly Trap for Indoors
reviewDescription: The FVOAI Fruit Fly Trap performs well for smaller flying insects and operates quietly in indoor spaces. It’s aesthetically pleasing and convenient to use. However, buyers should be aware of potential short-lived performance, limited suction strength, and the lack of robust customer support. Best for: Mild to moderate fruit-fly or gnat infestations. Not ideal if: You need long-term durability or stronger suction for larger flies.

It actually catches fruit flies. I had caught 20 in a couple hours. The fan needs to be slightly stronger for those stronger fruit flies; as this fan is extremely weak, you'd think it wouldn't work. This did not get rid of all the fruit flies, but it did capture a lot. If i had my choice I would add a stronger fan that allows different speeds to be chosen by the user and a larger hole for the bugs to fly through, as well as little cup/cap holder for holding a cap of apple cider vinegar to attract them - maybe something like the ketchup cups you get at fast food.. I'd also replace the sticky pads with a zapper, so you don't have to buy and throw away sticky pads all the time. Still a nice little bug catcher, but it definitely doesn't catch them all. So, this thing works, a little. The problem is, that while the noise level is relatively low, it is a horrible pitchy, whiney noise that you can't get away from. Kind of like a stuck electric motor or something. We have one from another brand, and it makes slightly more noise, but a normal fan noise that turns into background white noise, not this. It is unnatural, semi-high pitched, and insistent. I currently feel like I am living somewhere in between Edgar Allen Poe and HP Lovecraft's novels and am waiting for this insistent, persistent, gnawing sound to burst forth into something truly worthy of literary greatness. In other words, don't buy this one, get a different one.

reviewTitle: The plug to the machine snapped off.
reviewDescription: The machine worked fine until the power plug broke off. I was moving it to another area, unplugged the electrical socket portion, wrapped the power cord, plugged it into a new socket and it wasn't working. After inspecting it, I saw the USB portion broke off. I don't think I exerted an abnormal amount of force on it.

The food flytrap is disappointing and does not live up to the videos. It barely attracts or traps any flies, even when used as directed. The design seems ineffective, and it feels like a waste of time and money. Don’t be misled by the advertisements—this product falls short.

I guess my opinion of what quiet is and the manufacturer’s are different. This is NOT quiet. It’s louder than our Roomba! Not sure how well it gets bugs or if I’ll ever really find out because I don’t want to run it at night (says works better in dark) and listen to that racket all night. Definitely leaning toward returning due to the noise.
This product does not work! Don’t waste your money on it. It’s made with cheap plastic material. It is not a good value for your money.

At first it was working really good. Then I noticed that even though the light was on the fan wasn't working. I have to turn it off then turn it back on for the fan to start working. After a few minutes the fan will stop again.

This product did not perform as advertised. I have over 100 fruit flies on my ceiling every night and not one entered this contraption. I think that it is fundamentally not a sound concept. I would not buy this one again nor would I purchase a competitor's product that would have a similar concept. Very disappointed.

reviewTitle: great idea but lasts only 3 mo
reviewDescription: I purchased this item June 5th, 2024 and now August 17 this item turns on for maybe 20 minutes and then turns off. I have tried contacting seller (amazon in this case but it looks like this order is not eligible for tech support. So I would NOT recommend to buy this due to longevity of a product.

reviewTitle: So far in a month, only caught 3 big bugs
reviewDescription: Definitely was excited to try this product but the results were very sad. Needs to have either stronger suction power or something that brings in a greater attraction to these little fruit flies/mosquitos. Overall wish I could return for a refund. Guess I’m gonna have to handle these insects the good ole fashion way 👋
No es eficiente.

The product does not work as they say. It never helped with the problem. And just barely after a month. It’s completely stopped working. I called Amazon and the window to return the item had closed. I asked if there was a way of contacting the seller, they couldn’t find anything, I didn’t find anything other than a “physical address ” in China, no one mail no phone number, so I am stuck with this unit that does NOT work at all and I cannot get my money back. I cannot get it repaired, so here I am stuck with an item that doesn’t work.
If I could give it a 0 starts I would . I had it a little over a month and it died .

reviewTitle: The gnats are flying around it
reviewDescription: I bought this because it was only a few dollars more than the plug that came with one sticky pad and the reviews/pictures showed how much it was catching. However, my pet gnats have been flying around it. The video was yesterday and today has been just over 48 hours… not a single one, even with the ACV on the paper towel. And as you can see, the little piece of paper isn’t even budging so I’m not sure if I received a defective one or what… very sad.

reviewTitle: It may work, I can't tell because it's missing parts.
reviewDescription: I ordered this to help get rid of fruit flies. The system I received appears to be used and is missing components (like 9 of 10 of the glue pads) and didn't have a USB power cable if it was supposed to. The one glue pad that the trap had was used and included bugs! After hooking it up to one of my USB cables, the light turns on and it appears to run. It’s quieter than the fans on my computer, so if this works it may be a good product. Currently, I don’t have any idea.

reviewTitle: Disappointing power of fan, which is weak and noisy
reviewDescription: I've owned another one of these almost exact models that lasted me 5 years, was quiet and had a powerful fan that sucked many bugs down into it. It finally died of old age a month ago so I ordered this one that looks Exactly the same as my old one but this one has a VERY Weak and Very Noisy Fan. Not good. I don't recommend this to anyone. I've tried it now for a few weeks and it's just doesn't do the job other than annoying me with the noise. I will be returning it.

Why did you pick this product vs others?: Says “Zap” in the product name…. But does not zap. It’s a very weak fan tube that directs the insect to an adhesive disc. The fly tape hanging next to it caught 5 times more fruit flys last night. Terrible product. Do not waste money or time.
It stopped working within 40 days! It was not doing a great job but here and there it will catch 2-3 plant flies a night. I can not come in contact with the seller. Buy something else, trust me!

I don’t understand what people are saying when they say it’s quiet just got it got it out of the box to set it up. It is so loud. I have it in the kitchen and can hear it loudly from my bedroom going back immediately so disappointed don’t waste your money on this go with the other company that just plugs in

This did not catch any fruit flies for me. I used it according to directions and turned out all of the lights over night. Tried on two separate occasions and didn't work. I had to go back to the old vinegar and dish soap in a jar method.

reviewTitle: It's big and heavy. I returned it.
reviewDescription: It might work properly but make sure you like it size.
It said it traps gnats also, but I didn't find this to be true
The machine functions but does not entice all of the fruit flies!
This definitely captures some flying bugs but not really worth the money imo.
Could’ve been a little bit better
A little too big for me.
works okay but gnats are persistent

reviewTitle: Nice to look at, not very useful
reviewDescription: Worked well for the 1st week and then did not capture any more fruit flies. It is not worth the counter space
Worked like a champ for a couple of months, but then didn’t turn on. Changed charging chord as some other reviewers suggested. Working again. Fingers crossed.

reviewTitle: Don't let it run non stop
reviewDescription: Worked great got rid of my fly problem after moving into a older home. Fan broke tho after having it for 1 summer
